title: xylia xylocarpa
intro: Xylia xylocarpa, also known as the Indian beech tree or beechwood tree, is a deciduous tree native to the tropical forests of India and Southeast Asia. It is valued for its medicinal properties, primarily due to the presence of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids in its bark, leaves, and seeds, which exhibit anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Xylia xylocarpa include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and exert anti-cancer properties, making it a valuable herbal remedy for various ailments. Xylia xylocarpa can be prepared as a decoction, infusion, or ointment, and it is often used in traditional Ayurvedic medicine to treat conditions such as fever, digestive issues, and skin disorders.
